data.executiveSummary: The qa-login service faced a critical incident due to Redis connection timeouts impacting the session store retrieval process, resulting in a high error rate of 30.21%. Memory and CPU usage were within normal ranges, pointing towards the connection timeout as the primary issue.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeouts
rootCause.description: The incident was caused by repeated connection timeouts when the qa-login service attempted to retrieve data from the Redis session store, consistently failing after 5000ms.

evidenceChain: Observation of repeated 'LoginException' logs indicating 'Session store retrieval failure' due to 'Redis connection timeout after 5000ms'.
Prometheus metrics showed the CPU and memory usage were within normal bounds, with CPU at 19.70% and memory at 44.86%, suggesting no resource exhaustion.

timeline.event: LoginException in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.
LoginException in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.

data.impact: Users experienced failures when attempting to log in as the qa-login service could not successfully retrieve session information due to Redis connection issues, resulting in a high error rate.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Investigate and rectify the causes of Redis connection timeouts, specifically focusing on network issues or Redis resource saturation.
Implement monitoring for Redis connection health to preemptively detect and mitigate similar issues.
Consider restarting the Redis instance similar to the resolution of incident KAN-6 to clear potential orphaned connections.
